sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "The presenter described cyclic immunofluorescence\u2013based high\u2011plex 3D microscopy (up to 150 channels) applied to thick (35\u202f\u00b5m) FFPE sections and light\u2011sheet fluorescence imaging, enabling whole\u2011cell morphology, subcellular organelle localization, and spatial context (e.g., cytotoxic synapse distance, tertiary lymphoid structure architecture) to be captured in three dimensions, which resolves misidentification and provides functional readouts that are impossible in standard 2D sections.", "key_findings": ["Thick (35\u201140\u202f\u00b5m) FFPE sections retain ~70\u202f% of cells intact versus <5\u202f% in standard 5\u202f\u00b5m sections, dramatically reducing lineage misidentification.", "3D imaging revealed that ~40\u202f% of CD8+ T cells in tumors exhibit LAG\u20113 staining only in deeper z\u2011planes, explaining false\u2011negative checkpoint marker detection in 2D slides.", "Morphological analysis showed average T cells make 5\u201310 functional contacts with neighbors; synapse distances contract from ~1\u202f\u00b5m to ~30\u202fnm during cytolytic engagement.", "Serial reconstruction of ~470 tertiary lymphoid structures in lung adenocarcinoma and tuberculosis demonstrated heterogeneous developmental stages within a single follicle, which 2D sections misclassify.", "Light\u2011sheet 3D imaging of a 2\u202fmm\u2011thick colon specimen captured ~30\u202fmillion cells, allowing tracing of nerve\u2011immune interactions and vascular\u2011associated immune niches."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "The talk outlined how foundation models, segmentation networks, synthetic\u2011staining GANs and diffusion models, and optical\u2011flow based slice interpolation are applied to 2D and 3D histopathology data (H&E, cyclic IF, light\u2011sheet) to generate cell\u2011level segmentations, virtual multiplex stains, and fill missing z\u2011planes, thereby enabling quantitative morphometric and spatial analyses that were previously infeasible.", "key_findings": ["Foundation models can embed H&E tiles into high\u2011dimensional vectors that predict patient outcomes and highlight prognostic regions such as venous invasion without additional training.", "A GAN\u2011based pipeline converts quantitative phase images into realistic H&E, allowing existing H&E\u2011trained models to be applied to label\u2011free imaging.", "Diffusion models successfully synthesize multiplex IHC (e.g., insulin/glucagon) from a single H&E slide, enabling retrospective multi\u2011omic analysis of archived specimens.", "Optical\u2011flow algorithms reconstruct missing intermediate sections in CODA and Visium datasets, improving continuity for 3D reconstructions and spatial transcriptomics.", "AI\u2011driven nucleus and tissue segmentation using models like Segment\u2011Anything achieve >90\u202f% concordance with manual annotations across multiple organ systems."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "John Ifraty reviewed how AI image embeddings derived from H&E slides can capture morphologic correlates of actionable mutations (e.g., ALK fusions, MSI, EGFR) and discussed the need for clinical\u2011grade performance (high sensitivity, specificity, PPV). He highlighted that current AI models achieve AUROC ~0.9 for MSI but only ~0.6\u20110.7 for most other mutations, emphasizing confounders, sample quality, and rare tumor entities as major barriers. He also described emerging multimodal and spatial platforms that combine H&E, multiplex immunofluorescence, and spatial transcriptomics to generate richer biomarkers, while noting throughput and reproducibility challenges.", "key_findings": ["Clinical ALK FISH assay demonstrated 100% sensitivity, 99% specificity, and 100% PPV in validation against RNA\u2011Seq, setting a high bar for AI\u2011based predictors.", "AI model trained on public colorectal cancer slides achieved AUROC ~0.9 for MSI prediction, the highest performance among mutation\u2011prediction tasks.", "Predictive performance for EGFR, KRAS, PIK3CA, and other mutations ranged from AUROC 0.5\u20110.7, indicating limited clinical utility at present.", "Foundation models fine\u2011tuned on institution\u2011specific data (e.g., Campanella et al.) can raise EGFR prediction AUROC to ~0.9 and enable triage of lung cancer cases for rapid molecular testing.", "ctDNA assay (Guardant360) identified ALK fusions in only ~50% of cases, illustrating that sub\u2011optimal sensitivity can still be useful for longitudinal monitoring.", "Spatial multiplex IF and transcriptomics platforms (e.g., Singular Genomics 18\u2011plex IF + 500\u2011plex RNA) now achieve 10 samples per slide throughput, moving toward clinical feasibility."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Platz integrates population\u2011based epidemiology with pathology and molecular assays to show that intraprostatic inflammation, driven in part by infections such as CMV, increases prostate cancer risk; telomere length variability in cancer cells combined with short stromal telomeres predicts lethal disease; and long\u2011term use of statins or digoxin is inversely associated with aggressive prostate cancer, suggesting drug\u2011repurposing opportunities.", "key_findings": ["Intraprostatic inflammation measured in unbiased end\u2011of\u2011study biopsies is prospectively associated with increased prostate cancer risk (dose\u2011response with percent inflamed area).", "CMV seropositivity is positively associated with greater intraprostatic inflammation in the PCPT cohort.", "High variability of telomere length among cancer cells together with short stromal telomeres confers a 14\u2011fold higher risk of prostate cancer death, replicated at ~5\u2011fold in independent cohorts.", "Long\u2011term statin use is linked to a ~50% reduction in metastatic prostate cancer and death, independent of cholesterol levels.", "Long\u2011term digoxin use shows a similar inverse association with both organ\u2011confined and lethal prostate cancer.", "Lower stromal telomere length correlates with reduced lamin B1 expression, supporting a senescent fibroblast hypothesis."], "novelty_flag": "clinical_validation_of_known", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

Emerging targets (CCR8, mesothelin) showed limited efficacy, highlighting resistance mechanisms.", "key_findings": ["Zoldonrasib achieved a 52% ORR and median PFS of 11\u202fmonths in KRAS G12D mutant NSCLC (n=40) with only minimal grade\u202f3 toxicities.", "Deraxone rasib combined with gemcitabine/nab\u2011paclitaxel yielded a 59% ORR and 90% disease control in first\u2011line metastatic KRAS\u2011mutant pancreatic cancer, albeit with 73% grade\u202f3+ adverse events.", "Elacestrant produced a 32% partial response rate in KRAS G12C NSCLC patients who had progressed on prior G12C inhibitors.", "VE\u20111 plus PKMYT inhibition resulted in a 60% ORR in FBXW7\u2011mutant ovarian cancer harboring cyclin\u202fE1 amplification, demonstrating target\u2011driven efficacy.", "Sequential combination of trastuzumab deruxtecan with olaparib achieved a 54% overall response rate, supporting schedule\u2011optimized ADC\u2011PARP synergy.", "Claudin\u20116 and folate receptor ADCs each generated >50% response rates across multiple tumor types, even in low\u2011expressing cohorts.", "Cilta\u2011cel induced 100% MRD negativity and objective responses in high\u2011risk smoldering myeloma with no grade\u202f3+ CRS, indicating curative potential in pre\u2011malignancy."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "The work introduces universal allogeneic CAR\u2011T platforms that multiplex\u2011edit donor T cells to delete CD7 (and TCR\u03b1) for T\u2011ALL and to delete CD2 (and TCR\u03b1) for T\u2011cell lymphomas, enabling potent antigen\u2011specific killing while avoiding fratricide. sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Single\u2011cell genomic and transcriptomic analyses of bone\u2011marrow disseminated tumor cells (DTCs) from early\u2011stage (M0) and metastatic (M1) patients reveal that M0 DTCs lack late\u2011stage driver mutations, exhibit high stemness and embryonic\u2011like gene expression (similar to zygote/early\u2011embryo signatures), and their stemness scores strongly predict poor survival, indicating that early dissemination involves a primitive, pluripotent\u2011like state distinct from overt metastases.", "key_findings": ["Dissemination of tumor cells occurs early, often before a detectable primary tumor, in both mouse models and patients.", "M0 DTCs display embryonic stem\u2011cell\u2013like transcriptional programs and highest stemness scores, exceeding those of adult erythroid progenitors.", "High stemness scores in EpCAM+ DTCs correlate with markedly reduced overall survival in lung cancer patients.", "M0 DTCs cluster transcriptionally with human embryonic stem cells, whereas M1 DTCs align with differentiated epithelial/mesenchymal signatures.", "Phospho\u2011protein profiling of DTCs from a HER2\u2011positive patient after neoadjuvant therapy showed persistent activation of HER2, AKT, ERK, and STAT3 pathways, indicating therapy\u2011resistant signaling."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Recursion leveraged AI\u2011driven phenomaps to identify RBM39 as a functional analog of CDK12, enabling rapid degrader development (REC\u20111245) that exploits splicing defects in MSI\u2011high, genomically unstable tumors; AI\u2011guided design produced a CDK7 inhibitor (REC\u2011617) optimized for ovarian cancer vulnerability; and AI\u2011derived repurposing identified a MEK1/2 inhibitor (REC\u20114881) that reduces polyp burden in APC\u2011loss FAP patients.", "key_findings": ["RBM39 degradation by REC\u20111245 showed dose\u2011dependent activity in MSI\u2011high ovarian models and correlated with genomic instability across multiple preclinical models.", "REC\u20111245 entered the DAHLIA Phase 1/2 trial targeting MSI\u2011high and other high\u2011instability tumor types, with dose escalation ongoing.", "REC\u2011617 demonstrated in vitro ovarian cell line growth inhibition and in vivo tumor regressions; causal modeling of >10,000 Tempus records linked high CDK7 expression to poor survival, guiding ovarian cancer selection.", "A partial response was observed in an ovarian cancer patient treated with REC\u2011617 in the ELUCIDATE trial.", "REC\u20114881 achieved a 75% response rate and 43% median polyp burden reduction after 12 weeks in FAP patients, with 82% maintaining response (53% reduction) after a drug\u2011free interval."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "XCell extends the first\u2011generation scGBT foundation model by training a diffusion\u2011based language model on massive, diverse perturbation scRNA\u2011seq datasets (Pisces) to learn causal gene\u2011expression responses, while BioReason Pro aligns DNA\u2011 or protein\u2011sequence foundation models with large\u2011language\u2011model reasoning to predict pathway or GO\u2011term functions, using reinforcement learning and multimodal tokenization.", "key_findings": ["Training on 26\u202fM perturbed cells across 16 biological contexts enabled XCell to recover known protein\u2011protein interaction networks and improve causal inference over descriptive models.", "Scaling XCell from 50\u202fM to 5\u202fB parameters yielded a monotonic decrease in training loss and increase in test\u2011set accuracy, establishing a clear scaling law for virtual\u2011cell models.", "BioReason Pro achieved >80\u202f% of predictions judged superior to human expert annotations on 200 novel proteins, outperforming baseline methods across GO\u2011term categories.", "Both XCell and BioReason Pro were released open\u2011source; within two weeks, tens of thousands of users accessed the models, indicating strong community uptake."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Clapper presented mRNA\u2011based vaccines targeting EGFR L858R and KRAS G12C mutations found in premalignant lung ground\u2011glass opacities, demonstrating CD8+ T\u2011cell activation and reduced tumor formation in mouse models. She also described the small\u2011molecule p53 reactivator REM\u2011491, which restores transcriptional activity of multiple hotspot p53 mutants (R175H, R273) in vitro (EC50 ~40\u202fnM) and suppresses tumor growth in xenograft models without observable toxicity.", "key_findings": ["In a cohort of Lynch syndrome individuals, 85% maintained a positive IFN\u2011\u03b3 ELISpot response one year after two doses of the 209\u2011peptide NU209 vaccine.", "Whole\u2011exome sequencing of 200 ground\u2011glass lung nodules revealed 25% harbor EGFR L858R and 40% KRAS G12C mutations.", "EGFR L858R mRNA vaccine achieved >90% lipid nanoparticle encapsulation efficiency and induced robust CD8+ T\u2011cell responses in mice.", "Mice vaccinated with EGFR L858R mRNA vaccine showed a dramatic reduction in tumor formation compared with empty nanoparticle controls.", "REM\u2011491 induced Nox and Puma transcription in TP53\u2011mutant Cal\u201133 cells but not in WT or p53\u2011null cells, confirming mutant\u2011specific activity.", "REM\u2011491 achieved EC50 \u224840\u202fnM for mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization in TP53 R175H cells and lower EC50 in heterozygous R273 cells.", "In vivo, REM\u2011491 administered daily for 17 days reduced tumor volume and weight in TP53 R175H breast cancer xenografts without weight loss or biochemical toxicity, and MTD >400\u202fmg/kg was not reached."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Gina demonstrated that Nrf2 activation rewires cysteine and glutathione metabolism, creating dependencies on cystine uptake, glutamine metabolism, and NADPH production; she showed tissue-specific cysteine sourcing via transsulfuration in liver versus reliance on extracellular cystine in lung tumors, and highlighted how microenvironmental nutrient gradients shape these vulnerabilities.", "key_findings": ["Liver tumors retain transsulfuration activity, while KRAS-driven lung tumors lack cysteine synthesis and depend on extracellular cystine.", "Interstitial fluid of lung tumors contains ~40 \u00b5M reduced cysteine and ~1 mM reduced glutathione, indicating a shift to cysteine uptake.", "Blocking cystine uptake with GGSTOP impairs tumor growth in vivo, but technical issues limited chronic dosing.", "Nrf2-active cells become glutamate\u2011limited and are sensitized to glutaminase inhibition (CB\u2011839) in standard RPMI but not in tumor\u2011derived sTIF media.", "sTIF media, mimicking interstitial fluid, rescues cystine\u2011starved cells and abolishes CB\u2011839 sensitivity, revealing context\u2011dependent metabolic vulnerabilities."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Ho showed that chronic antigen exposure in tumors leads to accumulation of depolarized mitochondria in CD8 T cells; blocked mitophagy forces proteasomal degradation of mitochondrial proteins, releasing heme that binds and degrades BACH2, relieving repression of BLIMP1 and driving terminal exhaustion.", "key_findings": ["MDR\u2011low CD8 T cells in tumors have high mitochondrial mass but low membrane potential and upregulated proteasome genes.", "Proteasome inhibition (MG132) restores BACH2 and reduces BLIMP1, partially reversing exhaustion markers.", "Heme released from degraded mitochondrial hemoproteins increases in exhausted T cells and correlates with exhaustion severity in vivo.", "Exogenous heme treatment in vitro drives PD\u20111 up\u2011regulation, TCF1 loss, and cytokine production decline.", "BACH2 mutant resistant to heme binding preserves stemness markers even in tumor\u2011derived damaged\u2011mitochondria T cells."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Germline mutations in telomere maintenance genes (e.g., TINF2, RTEL1, TERT) cause critically short or dysfunctional telomeres, leading to stem\u2011cell failure and a strong selective pressure for clonal hematopoiesis; specific somatic mutations (TP53 loss, MDM4 overexpression, U2AF1) further drive malignant transformation, resulting in markedly elevated risks of head\u2011and\u2011neck SCC, AML, and MDS.", "key_findings": ["Median overall survival of the entire TBD cohort was 52\u202fyears, but carriers of biallelic or X\u2011linked/TINF2 variants had median survival only in the 30s.", "Overall cancer risk was ~3\u2011fold higher than the general population, rising to ~4\u2011fold before transplant.", "Head\u2011and\u2011neck squamous cell carcinoma risk was ~50\u2011fold higher (median onset age 14.7\u202fyears) in TINF2 mutation carriers.", "AML risk was ~50\u2011fold and MDS risk ~500\u2011fold higher than expected in age\u2011sex matched controls.", "Clonal hematopoiesis with non\u2011cancer\u2011associated mutations (POT1, TERT promoter, PPM1D) correlated with better prognosis, whereas TP53 loss, MDM4 overexpression, or U2AF1 mutations predicted cancer development.", "Telomere length measured by flow cytometry reliably distinguishes TBD patients from the general population and aids genotype\u2011phenotype counseling."], "novelty_flag": "clinical_validation_of_known", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Chromosomal instability creates aneuploidy\u2011induced stresses (DNA damage, proteotoxicity, metabolic imbalance) that generate synthetic lethal dependencies such as KIF18A, CDC20, RAF signaling, SCD1, and Aurora B; targeting these dependencies selectively kills CIN\u2011high tumor cells while sparing diploid normal cells.", "key_findings": ["KIF18A identified as a unique synthetic lethal vulnerability of chromosomally unstable aneuploid tumor cells across multiple independent studies.", "CDC20 loss sensitizes CIN\u2011positive cells to spindle assembly checkpoint inhibition.", "Aneuploid cells exhibit basal DNA damage and chronic MAPK (RAF) activation; sub\u2011lethal RAF inhibition resensitizes them to DNA\u2011damaging chemotherapy.", "Proteasome inhibition is more effective in highly aneuploid tumors when patients are retrospectively stratified by aneuploidy score.", "Loss of chromosome 17p (mono\u2011allelic TP53 loss) creates dependence on SCD1 for fatty\u2011acid synthesis in brain\u2011metastatic breast cancer, and SCD1 inhibitors show potent in vitro, ex\u2011vivo, and in vivo activity.", "Aurora B kinase expression is down\u2011regulated in 17p\u2011loss tumors, rendering them hypersensitive to alvespib, a selective Aurora B inhibitor.", "ARGOS gene RBM14, located near CCND1 amplifications, is toxic when overexpressed and its amplification correlates with worse prognosis."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "KIF18A inhibition exploits a synthetic lethal interaction with RB\u2011pathway loss in CIN\u2011high tumors, while chronic cGAS\u2011STING signaling in aneuploid cancers desensitizes interferon output; transient cGAS inhibition restores STING protein levels and re\u2011sensitizes tumors to STING agonists, enabling acute interferon\u2011driven anti\u2011tumor immunity.", "key_findings": ["KIF18A inhibitors (VLS\u20111488, savolnisib) show clinical activity only in p16\u2011positive (RB\u2011deficient) tumors, with partial responses in all p16\u2011positive cases except one mixed\u2011histology outlier.", "p16 IHC serves as a robust predictive biomarker for KIF18A inhibitor response across multiple tumor types and dose levels.", "RB loss drives MAD2 overexpression, creating the synthetic lethal context required for KIF18A inhibitor efficacy.", "In chromosomally unstable tumors, chronic cGAS\u2011STING activation suppresses type\u2011I interferon via STING degradation and ER\u2011stress induction, rendering STING agonists ineffective.", "Pharmacologic cGAS inhibition (RU521) or genetic cGAS knockout restores STING levels, re\u2011activates interferon signaling, and markedly enhances response to STING agonists in mouse models.", "High cGAS / low STING expression in pretreatment biopsies predicts lack of response to STING agonists in a head\u2011and\u2011neck clinical cohort."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "phase_1"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Claudin\u201118.2 ADCs deliver high\u2011potency cytotoxic payloads (e.g., topoisomerase inhibitors) directly to antigen\u2011positive tumor cells, while bispecific T\u2011cell engagers (2+1 format) recruit CD3\u2011positive T cells to form immune synapses independent of HLA, enabling cytotoxicity even at moderate antigen density and providing bystander killing of neighboring claudin\u2011negative cells.", "key_findings": ["ASP2138 ADC induced potent killing across gastric cancer cell lines with varying claudin\u201118.2 density, though higher expression correlated with greater potency.", "Claudin\u201118.2 bispecific TCE showed cytotoxicity in vitro even at low antigen levels and mediated bystander killing in mixed claudin\u2011positive/negative organoid cultures.", "Pre\u2011clinical data suggest that lower CD3 affinity bispecifics can retain efficacy while potentially reducing cytokine release syndrome risk.", "Clinical data from an Astellas claudin\u201118.2 ADC (phase\u202f3) demonstrate activity comparable to HER2\u2011targeted ADCs, supporting the therapeutic window in gastric cancer.", "Heterogeneous claudin expression (\u224887% of gastric tumors) underscores the need for strategies such as dual\u2011antigen targeting or bystander effects to overcome antigen loss."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Claudin\u201118.2 CAR\u2011T cells are engineered to recognize the extracellular loop of claudin\u201118.2, leading to antigen\u2011specific T\u2011cell activation, cytokine release, and tumor cell lysis; persistence is limited by rapid exhaustion, prompting strategies such as naive T\u2011cell enrichment, novel lymphodepletion, and repeat infusions to improve durability.", "key_findings": ["Monotherapy claudin\u201118.2 CAR\u2011T achieved a 55% ORR and \u226519% disease control rate in gastric cancer.", "Sequential treatment (chemo\u202f\u2192\u202fPD\u20111\u202f\u2192\u202fCAR\u2011T) yielded an 80% ORR and 15\u2011month median PFS, suggesting benefit of tumor debulking before CAR\u2011T.", "Randomized trial showed a four\u2011fold higher response rate and a two\u2011fold OS improvement versus standard therapy, reducing mortality risk by 63%.", "High claudin\u201118.2 expression, absence of liver/bone metastases, and a TZMK\u207a/TPX\u207a immune\u2011rich microenvironment predicted long\u2011term responders.", "Grade\u202f\u22653 gastric mucosal injury occurred in ~10% of patients but was manageable with PPIs and fasting; pulmonary toxicity was rare and linked to CRS.", "Repeat CAR\u2011T infusion showed diminished expansion and persistence, highlighting the need for improved persistence strategies."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Reznik's group defined cachexia windows using longitudinal weight loss criteria in EHR data, aligned these windows with serologic, imaging, and genomic measurements, and identified three CT-based anatomical subtypes (A, B, C) distinguished by organ volume changes and systemic inflammation, with TP53 mutations emerging as a genomic risk factor when modeled as a time-to-event.", "key_findings": ["Implemented a 5% weight loss over 6\u2011month EHR algorithm to define cachexia windows in >60,000 cancer patients.", "Found ~30% prevalence of cachexia episodes in melanoma and high prevalence in pancreatic cancer.", "Identified three CT\u2011based cachexia subtypes: Type A (fat loss + spleen/liver enlargement), Type B (fat loss + organ atrophy and radiodensity increase), Type C (mixed).", "Type A subtype associated with worst overall survival across diseases.", "TP53 mutation is the most significant genomic correlate of cachexia risk when modeled as a time\u2011to\u2011event, validated in an independent cohort.", "Type A patients exhibit rising NLR and dropping albumin during cachexia episodes, indicating systemic inflammation."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Dunn presented the design and early baseline findings of two prospective observational cohorts (Lotus and UNCOVER) that longitudinally collect patient-reported outcomes, body composition imaging, and blood/tumor biomarkers to identify cachexia phenotypes; preliminary symptom clustering revealed high\u2011symptom and low\u2011symptom groups, with the high\u2011symptom group showing elevated GDF15, IL\u20116, and IL\u20118 and worse early disease progression.", "key_findings": ["Enrolled ~1,000 patients in Lotus (U Rochester) and ~800 in UNCOVER (Kaiser) to prospectively phenotype cachexia.", "Baseline: >50% of patients already have \u22655% weight loss; pancreatic cancer patients show highest prevalence.", "Symptom clustering identified three groups: low symptom burden (similar to weight\u2011stable), high symptom burden (fatigue, anorexia, emotional distress), and intermediate.", "High symptom burden cluster associated with higher serum GDF15, IL\u20116, and IL\u20118 levels.", "Patients in high symptom burden cluster had lower physical activity, higher appetite stimulant use, and higher 3\u2011month disease progression rates.", "Preliminary data suggest that symptom clusters may correlate with body\u2011composition clusters (e.g., low symptom = minimal wasting)."], "novelty_flag": "clinical_validation_of_known", "clinical_stage": "phase_2"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Gonsalves demonstrated that elevated prostaglandin E2 production by tumor\u2011associated neutrophils and tumor cells drives cachexia and impairs anti\u2011PD\u2011L1 efficacy; CRISPR\u2011mediated knockdown of PTGES or PTGS2 reduces PGE2, delays weight loss in KRAS/LKB1 mouse models and C26 allografts, linking PGE2 to both wasting and immunotherapy resistance, with dysbiotic lung microbiota further amplifying neutrophil activation.", "key_findings": ["In KRAS/LKB1 (KL) lung cancer mice, ~80% develop cachexia with up to 20% weight loss; non\u2011cachectic littermates retain weight despite similar tumor burden.", "Cachectic mice show higher tumor and serum IL\u20116 family cytokines and enrichment of arginase\u2011positive macrophages and prostaglandin\u2011producing neutrophils.", "Prostaglandin E2 levels are markedly increased in cachectic mouse tumors and in bronchoalveolar lavage fluid of cachectic lung cancer patients.", "CRISPR knockdown of PTGES in KL tumors suppresses PGE2 and significantly delays cachexia onset.", "CRISPR knockout of PTGS2 in C26 cachectic cells eliminates tumor PGE2 and protects mice from weight loss.", "Lung tumor dysbiosis (higher 16S density) correlates with cachexia and neutrophil infiltration; introducing dysbiotic bacteria to non\u2011cachectic tumor\u2011bearing mice induces weight loss.", "Clinical cohort analysis shows cachexia reversal after ICI correlates with high PD\u2011L1 expression and partial responses, whereas continued cachexia associates with progressive disease and early death."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Jaiswal introduced the PACER (passenger approximated clonal expansion rate) method to infer clone age from passenger mutations, identified TCL1A promoter SNP rs2887399 as a regulator of clonal fitness, and demonstrated that ectopic TCL1A expression drives HSC expansion and myeloid leukemia in transgenic mice, establishing TCL1A as a causal driver of CHIP.", "key_findings": ["PACER can quantify clonal expansion rates from bulk WGS data using passenger mutation counts.", "Driver mutation identity accounts for most variance in clone fitness; splicing factor mutations (U2AF1, SRSF2, SF3B1) and JAK2 show highest fitness.", "Smoking is the only measured environmental factor positively correlated with clonal expansion rate in humans.", "GWAS identified rs2887399 in the TCL1A promoter; the T allele slows expansion of multiple CHIP drivers.", "TCL1A is normally silent in HSCs; driver mutations de\u2011repress TCL1A, leading to HSC fitness advantage.", "Transgenic mice with HSC\u2011specific TCL1A expression develop HSPC expansion and 100% mortality from myeloid leukemia by 12\u201113 months.", "Carriers of the protective T allele have reduced risk of myeloid malignancies, especially CMML."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.prompt_json: {"talk_id": "clonal-hematopoiesis-aging-and-drivers-of-blood-cancer-and-solid-tumors::nangalia::2", "session": "clonal-hematopoiesis-aging-and-drivers-of-blood-cancer-and-solid-tumors", "speaker": "Jyoti Nangalia", "affiliation": "Cambridge University Hospital & Wellcome Sanger Institute", "tumor_types": ["myeloid malignancy", "breast cancer"], "clinical_stage": "preclinical", "task": "extract_competitive_intelligence", "instruction": "Extract the key scientific claims, clinical data, and competitive positioning from this oncology conference presentation."}
sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Nangalia described a combined whole\u2011genome and whole\u2011methylome sequencing pipeline for single\u2011cell colonies, enabling reconstruction of somatic mutation phylogenies and assignment of CpG methylation changes to specific ancestral branches, revealing massive early\u2011life epiallelic remodeling and that driver mutations both shape and are shaped by these epigenetic landscapes, with some epigenetic changes acting as oncogenic drivers themselves.", "key_findings": ["Developed an automated low\u2011input pipeline to obtain both WGS and whole\u2011genome methylomes from ~2\u202fng DNA per colony.", "Mapped ~5,000 CpG\u2011segment epialleles that change early in embryogenesis (\u224810\u201315 somatic mutations) with ~10,000\u2011fold higher rate than later life.", "Identified ~4,000\u20135,000 early\u2011life CpG segments (mostly promoters) that acquire stable methylation states, many showing allele\u2011specific patterns beyond X\u2011inactivation.", "Demonstrated that CHIP driver mutations (e.g., DNMT3A, TET2, 1q+, DNMT3A Y66F) are associated with increased numbers of epimutations per branch.", "Showed that epigenetic silencing of one BRCA1 allele can occur pre\u2011gastrulation, creating a mosaic that predisposes to BRCA\u2011like breast cancer.", "Found that somatic methylation changes can act as drivers, with certain embryonic epialleles preferentially selected by specific mutations.", "Established that methylation trees can resolve polytomies where SNV trees cannot, providing finer temporal resolution of early clonal events."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.prompt_json: {"talk_id": "clonal-hematopoiesis-aging-and-drivers-of-blood-cancer-and-solid-tumors::ebert::3", "session": "clonal-hematopoiesis-aging-and-drivers-of-blood-cancer-and-solid-tumors", "speaker": "Ben Ebert", "affiliation": "Dana\u2011Farber Cancer Institute, Harvard Medical School", "tumor_types": ["myeloid malignancy", "cardiovascular disease", "chronic obstructive pulmonary disease", "liver disease", "lung cancer"], "clinical_stage": "mixed", "task": "extract_competitive_intelligence", "instruction": "Extract the key scientific claims, clinical data, and competitive positioning from this oncology conference presentation."}
sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Ebert showed that loss of TET2 in hematopoietic cells reprograms lipid metabolism, leading to accumulation of triglycerides, diacylglycerols and cholesterol esters, which drives heightened IL\u20111\u03b2\u2011mediated inflammation in atherosclerosis, COPD, NASH and other age\u2011related diseases; genetic screens identified ACLY and OGT as key mediators, and pharmacologic inhibition of lipid pathways normalizes the inflammatory phenotype.", "key_findings": ["CHIP carriers have increased risk of atherosclerosis, myocardial infarction, heart failure, arrhythmias, liver disease, kidney disease, COPD, osteoporosis and lung cancer.", "TET2 loss leads to lipid droplet accumulation in neutrophils/macrophages, driving IL\u20111\u03b2\u2011dependent inflammation.", "In vivo CRISPR screen in Tet2\u2011deficient mice identified ACLY and OGT as essential for the inflammatory phenotype; knockout of these genes restores wild\u2011type inflammation levels.", "TET2 directly binds OGT; loss of TET2 liberates OGT, causing aberrant protein O\u2011GlcNAcylation and metabolic dysregulation.", "A simple risk score incorporating genotype, clone size, RDW, MCV, cytopenias and age stratifies CHIP individuals into low, intermediate and high risk for myeloid malignancy and overall mortality.", "High\u2011risk CHIP (~1% of population) has ~50% overall survival, whereas low\u2011risk/CHIP\u2011negative individuals have ~95% survival.", "IL\u20111\u03b2 inhibition (e.g., canakinumab) reduces lung cancer incidence, suggesting inflammatory CHIP contributes to early tumorigenesis."], "novelty_flag": "best_in_class", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Leake outlined the current landscape of AI applications across oncology drug development, emphasizing the distinction between supervised and generative models, the regulatory uncertainty surrounding generative AI, and the need for robust benchmarks, representation, and guardrails to ensure safe, responsible deployment.", "key_findings": ["AI is now embedded in >1,500 FDA regulatory submissions over the past decade, with oncology accounting for ~27% of AI\u2011containing submissions.", "The FDA received 307 AI\u2011related oncology submissions in 2024 alone, reflecting rapid growth.", "Four major impact areas for AI in oncology were identified: care/operations, drug discovery, drug development, and consumer health.", "Generative AI models present greater regulatory challenges than supervised models due to broader use cases and lack of clear pathways.", "Benchmarking, representation across diverse populations, and external validation were highlighted as critical gaps to address."], "novelty_flag": "unspecified", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Visfilo described AstraZeneca's experience with AI\u2011enabled pathology devices, the three classes of AI\u2011driven biomarkers (replication, inference, outcome prediction), and advocated for third\u2011party benchmarking, clear guardrails, and high levels of evidence to ensure safe clinical deployment.", "key_findings": ["AstraZeneca achieved the first Class\u202f2 FDA clearance for an AI pathology device in 2021 and a breakthrough device designation for an AI\u2011derived predictive biomarker for an ADC.", "Three AI biomarker classes were defined: Class\u202fA (task replication), Class\u202fB (inferred biomarkers), Class\u202fC (prognostic or predictive outcomes).", "For Class\u202fC2 (predictive of therapeutic response), prospective trial validation is required; explainability is not mandatory if performance is demonstrated.", "Emphasis on multi\u2011stakeholder partnerships (academia, industry, payers, regulators, patients) to set evaluation standards and avoid a \u201cWild West\u201d scenario."], "novelty_flag": "unspecified", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "pks+ E. coli produces colibactin, a genotoxin that creates AT-rich DNA lesions (SBS88) and mutates APC splice sites in young patients; colibactin also binds AT-rich motifs, displacing CDX2 and repressing differentiation programs, thereby promoting a shift of epithelial cells toward an LGR5+ intestinal stem cell state, enhancing regenerative capacity and tumor initiation in the young colon.", "key_findings": ["Developed a targeted-exome assay to detect the colibactin SBS88 signature in >80,000 clinical tumors, finding enrichment in colorectal, esophageal, bladder, and head/neck cancers.", "Colibactin signature-positive tumors occur in significantly younger patients and are more often located in the rectosigmoid region.", "Identified a specific APC splice-site mutation occurring in the AT-rich colibactin context, enriched in young-onset colorectal cancers, suggesting colibactin-driven initiation.", "Inverted-polarity organoid model exposed to pks+ E. coli recapitulated colibactin mutational signatures and induced upregulation of LGR5 and ASCL2 stem cell markers.", "pks+ E. coli exposure increased functional stem cell capacity (organoid formation) and proliferation compared to \u0394pks controls.", "Thermal proteomics and ATAC-seq revealed colibactin-induced repression of AT-rich transcription factor motifs, notably CDX2, leading to loss of differentiation and promotion of stemness.", "Young colon organoids showed greater regenerative response to colibactin-induced injury than aged organoids, linking age-dependent stem cell capacity to early-onset tumorigenesis."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Compounds 15 and 64 act as molecular glues that bind the N\u2011terminal domain of AR/ARV7 and recruit a Cullin\u2011RING ligase complex (DDB1\u2011containing) to induce proteasomal degradation of both isoforms, thereby suppressing AR\u2011driven transcription and overcoming enzalutamide resistance.", "key_findings": ["High\u2011throughput screen of 170,000 compounds identified 13 hits; lead chemotype 7907 degraded both AR and ARV7 via proteasome.", "Medicinal chemistry optimization yielded analogs 15 and 64 with sub\u2011micromolar EC50s and rapid (\u22644\u202fh) degradation of both proteins.", "Compound 15 suppressed AR\u2011driven transcription in 22RV1 cells, outperforming enzalutamide and ARV110 in RNA\u2011seq AR\u2011score analysis.", "In vivo, oral dosing of compounds 15 and 64 markedly inhibited growth of ARV7\u2011positive 22RV1 xenografts, achieving greater tumor regression than enzalutamide or ARV110.", "Mechanistic studies mapped activity to the AR N\u2011terminal TAL1 and AF1 subdomains; degradation required Cullin\u2011RING ligase activity (blocked by MLN4924) and was partially reversed by DDB1 knockout.", "Global proteomics showed AR and ARV7 among the top\u2011down\u2011regulated proteins with minimal off\u2011target effects; compounds were well tolerated with no weight loss."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Sex- and ancestry-specific mutations (SPEN, Notch2, ATM, MGA, SETD2) reprogram B-cell differentiation toward aged, auto-reactive AIBCs, driving aggressive DLBCL phenotypes; female BN2 DLBCLs exhibit X-chromosome instability and heightened TLR7 signaling, rendering them sensitive to IRAK inhibition, while African ancestry DLBCLs display senescence-associated secretory phenotypes linked to SETD2 loss, suggesting precision therapeutic windows.", "key_findings": ["SPEN truncation co-occurs with Notch2 truncation in BN2 DLBCLs and predicts inferior survival in a 4,500-patient cohort.", "Female BN2 DLBCL mice develop X\u2011chromosome hypomethylation, increased TLR7 expression, and are more sensitive to IRAK inhibitors than males.", "African ancestry DLBCLs harbor frequent ATM, MGA, and SETD2 mutations (>50%) and show elevated senescence (GLB1) and exhausted CD4 T\u2011cell infiltrates.", "SETD2\u2011heterozygous DLBCL cells are hypersensitive to a clinical SETD2 inhibitor, offering a potential ancestry\u2011specific therapy.", "IRAK inhibition reduces tumor growth in female BN2 DLBCL xenografts, supporting a sex\u2011specific precision therapy approach."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "unspecified"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Aging reprograms breast cancer transcriptomes: older ER\u2011positive tumors show reduced immune and metabolic pathways, while older triple\u2011negative tumors acquire an inflamed, EMT\u2011driven phenotype; age\u2011selected subclones (e.g., barcode\u202f25) become resistant to paclitaxel, but are sensitized to HDAC inhibition combined with checkpoint blockade, revealing age\u2011specific therapeutic vulnerabilities.", "key_findings": ["Older breast cancer patients exhibit distinct molecular landscapes: ER+ tumors lose immune/metabolic activity, while TNBCs gain an inflamed EMT phenotype.", "In mouse models, aged mice show slower NET1 tumor growth but reduced response to paclitaxel\u202f+\u202fanti\u2011PD\u2011L1, despite higher CD8 infiltration and Treg expansion.", "Barcode\u202f25 subclone is enriched by age and paclitaxel, displays intrinsic paclitaxel resistance, and can be eliminated by HDAC inhibition.", "Panobinostat combined with anti\u2011PD\u2011L1 dramatically extends survival in aged mice, whereas the same combination is less effective in young mice without chemotherapy."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "mixed"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Cord\u2011blood derived allogeneic NK cells are engineered with an IL\u201115 armoured CD70\u2011CAR (or CD5\u2011CAR) and a safety switch; they expand robustly in patients, persist for months, and mediate tumor cell killing directly and via ADCC, while a bispecific engager (AFM13) redirects NK cells to CD30\u2011positive Hodgkin lymphoma, achieving >90% ORR.", "key_findings": ["Cord\u2011blood derived CD70 CAR\u2011NK cells from optimal units (frozen \u226424\u202fh, nucleated RBC\u202f\u2248\u202f80\u202fM) yielded 70% 1\u2011year PFS and 95% 1\u2011year OS, whereas delayed\u2011freeze units performed poorly.", "In a 42\u2011patient Hodgkin lymphoma study combining AFM13 with CAR\u2011NK, overall response rate exceeded 90% with two\u2011thirds achieving CR, despite lack of bridging therapy.", "CAR\u2011NK cells persisted up to 6\u202fmonths in peripheral blood, with early clonal expansion (clone\u202f0) followed by emergence of a memory\u2011like clone (clone\u202f4) linked to long\u2011term persistence.", "CD5 CAR\u2011NK therapy in T\u2011PLL achieved CR in 3 of 4 heavily pre\u2011treated patients, with donor NK cells tracking to disease clearance.", "Manufacturing from a single cord\u2011blood unit can produce 250 patient doses at <$600 per dose, enabling off\u2011the\u2011shelf scalability."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "phase_1"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Allogeneic CAR\u2011T cells are engineered to eliminate TCR (prevent GvHD) and CD52 (allow anti\u2011CD52 lymphodepletion), or to express HLA\u2011E and knock\u2011out \u03b22\u2011microglobulin (stealth), enabling robust expansion after standard lymphodepletion; CD70 is leveraged both as a tumor antigen and as a target to deplete alloreactive host T cells, improving persistence and efficacy in RCC and lymphoma.", "key_findings": ["In the MRD\u2011guided ALO\u2011501A trial, 7 of 8 patients cleared MRD after a single dose of allogeneic CD19 CAR\u2011T with standard flu\u2011cyclophosphamide lymphodepletion, without CRS/ICANS.", "CD70\u2011targeted allogeneic CAR\u2011T (LO316) achieved a 31% ORR in CD70\u2011high RCC, with durable responses >12\u202fmonths in most responders.", "Gene\u2011editing approaches (TCR knockout, CD52 knockout, HLA\u2011E insertion) enable allogeneic CAR\u2011T expansion comparable to autologous products despite HLA\u2011agnostic lymphodepletion.", "Selective depletion of alloreactive host T cells via CD70 targeting (the \u201cdagger\u201d approach) enhances CAR\u2011T persistence and reduces rejection."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "phase_1_2"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "ARCH-DR is a high\u2011throughput archival DNA\u2011RNA co\u2011sequencing chemistry that simultaneously captures copy\u2011number alterations, somatic mutations and full\u2011length transcriptomes from thousands of single cells in fresh, frozen or FFPE esophageal adenocarcinoma specimens, enabling reconstruction of genetic lineages and phenotypic plasticity.", "key_findings": ["Analyzed 12 EAC patients (\u224834,000 cells) with ARCH\u2011DR, revealing patient\u2011specific transcriptional clusters driven by copy\u2011number dosage effects.", "Identified three epithelial lineages (esophageal, gastric, intestinal) within individual tumors, with most subclones displaying mixed lineage phenotypes, indicating high cellular plasticity.", "Detected ecDNA amplifications of EGFR and ERBB2 that segregated with distinct genetic subclones and showed expression saturation at ~20 copies.", "Found that ~51% of genes exhibit dosage\u2011sensitive expression correlating with copy\u2011number changes, while the remainder are dosage\u2011insensitive.", "Observed that ~20% of somatic mutations are associated with altered gene expression, highlighting mutation\u2011driven transcriptional effects."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "Primary Template-directed Amplification (PTA) adds irreversible terminators to a Phi29\u2011based whole\u2011genome amplification, producing uniformly covered single\u2011cell genomes that retain accurate allele frequencies, enabling high\u2011sensitivity SNV and copy\u2011number detection, which was applied to pediatric leukemia to uncover a hidden mutational burden and clonal drug\u2011resistance mechanisms.", "key_findings": ["PTA achieves ~90% detection of known germline variants with allele\u2011frequency distribution centered at 50%, outperforming MDA and PCR\u2011based methods.", "Single\u2011cell sequencing of pediatric leukemia revealed 2,000\u20134,000 mutations per cell, far exceeding the <1,000 mutations observed in bulk sequencing.", "De\u2011novo mutational signatures (SBS2/13 APOBEC) were identified at the single\u2011cell level, showing heterogeneous signature activity across clones.", "Post\u2011treatment MRD analysis uncovered JAK2\u2011mutant subclones that were undetectable in bulk but expanded after chemotherapy, suggesting a genetic basis for resistance.", "Integration of surface\u2011marker index sorting with PTA enabled mapping of phenotypic (surface protein) and genotypic (SNV/CNV) traits onto phylogenetic trees."], "novelty_flag": "first_in_class", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}

sft_rows.remote_response_json: {"moa_summary": "SKY\u2011Met combines combinatorial indexing with nucleosome disruption to achieve genome\u2011wide bisulfite\u2011free single\u2011cell DNA methylation profiling at high throughput; the platform also supports simultaneous ATAC\u2011seq or whole\u2011genome sequencing, enabling multi\u2011omic interrogation of epigenetic state, chromatin accessibility and copy\u2011number in thousands of cells from frozen or fixed tissues.", "key_findings": ["Generated a 140,000\u2011cell methylome atlas of the human cerebral cortex, revealing cell\u2011type\u2011specific CpG methylation patterns and non\u2011CG methylation dynamics in neuronal subtypes.", "Applied SKY\u2011Met to pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, achieving >90% CpG coverage per cell and enabling copy\u2011number profiling from frozen tumor specimens.", "Demonstrated a joint methylome\u2011ATAC co\u2011assay on ~8% of 140,000 bone\u2011marrow cells, providing concordant cell\u2011type clustering from both modalities.", "Detected a balanced translocation in a PDAC subclone using combined single\u2011cell genome + Hi\u2011C reads, illustrating the ability to capture structural variants alongside epigenetic information."], "novelty_flag": "platform_technology", "clinical_stage": "preclinical"}
